angles.claim: The endgame egBoid is purely the NN's argmin; NO eg_bound cert / NO exact-scan fallback in the decision path; a malformed pick is penalized as a disagreement; and the planner (N>5) is verbatim prod (untouched).

findings.issue: egBoid is solely the NN argmin — no cert/scan branch in the deployed decision path. endgamePolicy.js:32 returns pick(...).egIdx; egboidPick.js:50-64 is argmin of scanTPred (the MLP forward) over present boids. Grep of the full deploy chain (endgamePolicy.js, egboidPick.js, eg_features.js) shows ZERO references to eg_scan/exact scanT/egPick/eg_bound or any t<=TMAX loop. eg_bound.js is referenced by NOTHING in deploy/decision/egnn. Claim component CONFIRMED.
findings.evidence: grep over /workspace/.team/exact-nn-endgame-student/{endgamePolicy.js,egboidPick.js,eg_features.js}: only scanTPred (NN). eg_bound referenced by nothing. predator_cheap.js:364-371 prod scan-block is skipped because the injected gate always sets egBoid in nn mode.

findings.issue: No exact-scan fallback in the decision path. The only eg_scan.egPick call lives in the MEASUREMENT harness (candidates/egnn.js:105), computing prodIdx purely for agreement scoring and to build the malformed-penalty index — never fed to the NN, never a decision fallback. In nn mode the gate never returns -1, so prod's if(!egBoid){scan...} block never fires. CONFIRMED.
findings.evidence: egnn.js:39 injects `if(!egBoid && __egnnGate){var __egi=__egnnGate(...); if(__egi>=0) egBoid=boids[__egi];}` BEFORE prod's scan block; nn-mode idx is always valid (line 114 maps malformed to a valid index), so __egi>=0 always.

findings.issue: Malformed pick IS penalized as a disagreement, not silently routed to prod. egnn.js:114 maps malformed -> (prodIdx+1)%n (guaranteed mismatch) for n>=2. Probe: a student returning idx=999 every call yielded 26/36 egDisagree, S_dec=27.778%, gate FAIL — definitively NOT silent prod fallback. Real model: 0 malformed over 59 (mobile scatter) + 18 (natural) commits.
findings.evidence: EXACTNN_EGNN_STUDENT=/tmp/malformed_student.js -> S_dec_endgame 0.277777778, egDisagree 26/36. Real model via egnn's own __egnnStatsLast: malformed=0.

findings.issue: Planner (N>5) is verbatim prod — the injection only touches the N<=5 endgame. Structurally the gate is inside intercept() (predator_cheap.js:392, reached only when boids.length<=5); planCheap (N>5, line 393) never sees it and never touches egBoid. Confirmed: natural oracle planDisagree=0/1285; real-NN natural 4 games planDisagree=0/2297, egDisagree=0/18, force mismatches=0.
findings.evidence: verdict_moe.js oracle natural: S_dec_planner=1, planDisagree_total=0 over plans_total=1285, planner force cos=rel=1. Direct nn lockstep: plans=2297 planDisagree=0, force mismatches=0.

findings.issue: Stats-global wiring bug: verdict_moe.js:108 reads global.__moeStatsLast, but egnn.js:125 sets global.__egnnStatsLast. So __moeStatsLast is UNDEFINED when egnn runs, and the `if(gs)` guard (verdict_moe.js:112) is falsy — making the evidence's gateMalformed_total:0 / gateFlips_total:0 a hardcoded artifact of reading the WRONG global, not a measured value. Proven: with the malformed student, egnn's own counter recorded malformed=3 while the verdict path would report 0. The 0-malformed claim is independently TRUE for the real model but its provenance in the verdict JSONs is broken. NOTE: this does NOT create a hidden fallback — malformed still surfaces as egDisagree (lower S_dec). Does not falsify my angle.
findings.evidence: probe: global.__moeStatsLast = undefined; global.__egnnStatsLast = {commits:3,malformed:3,...} for the malformed student. verdict_moe.js:108 `const gs = global.__moeStatsLast`.

findings.issue: soleN1 (single-boid trivial commits) inflate the endgame denominator and are NOT surfaced numerically in the egnn verdict. egnn tracks stats.soleN1 (egnn.js:101) but verdict_moe.js never reports it (only verdict_l1e.js does). ~28.8% of mobile-scatter commits are degenerate n==1 cases where any pick trivially equals prod's only boid. Disclosed only in PHASE2 prose, not in the sealed-evidence numbers.
findings.evidence: Real-model probe over 20 mobile scatter games: commits=59, soleN1=17 (28.8%). grep shows soleN1 reported only by verdict_l1e.js, not verdict_moe.js (the egnn driver).

angles.conclusion: My no-hidden-fallback angle SURVIVES. The deployed endgame decision is purely the NN's argmin over scanTPred (the MLP forward) — the deploy chain (endgamePolicy -> egboidPick -> eg_features) contains no exact-scan loop, no egPick, and no eg_bound cert; eg_bound.js is dead code referenced by nothing. The only eg_scan.egPick call lives in the measurement harness, never in the decision. In nn mode the gate always emits a valid index, so prod's own scan-fallback block never fires. A malformed pick is genuinely penalized as a disagreement (malformed student -> 27.8% S_dec, 26/36 egDisagree), not silently routed to prod; the real model emits 0 malformed. The planner (N>5) is byte-verbatim prod — the gate lives only inside intercept() (N<=5), and both the oracle control (planDisagree=0/1285) and the live NN (planDisagree=0/2297, force mismatches=0) confirm zero planner perturbation. The oracle control is 100.000%. TWO disclosed-but-non-fatal holes: (1) a stats-global wiring bug — verdict_moe.js reads __moeStatsLast (undefined for egnn, which sets __egnnStatsLast), so the evidence's gateMalformed/gateFlips=0 are hardcoded artifacts, not measurements (the 0-malformed claim happens to be true for the real model but its provenance is broken; importantly it does NOT enable a hidden fallback since malformed still counts as egDisagree); (2) ~28.8% of scatter commits are degenerate single-boid cases that trivially agree and inflate the endgame denominator, surfaced only in prose, not the verdict numbers. Neither hole creates a hidden cert/scan fallback or touches the planner, so the architectural claim under my angle holds; the two findings are measurement-integrity caveats best handed to the measurement-integrity angle.

angles.claim: The endgame NN predicts each boid's scan-t from 18-dim cheap closed-form geometry (eg_features), NOT from prod's exact O(N·TMAX) scan-t, and degrades gracefully under ablation — a genuine learned predictor, not a single-value passthrough or an exact-answer relay.

findings.issue: No exact scan-t feeds the net — source audit of the full decision path confirms closed-form O(1) features only
findings.evidence: Decision path endgamePolicy.js -> egboidPick.js -> eg_features.js. eg_features.js (lines 15-76) is pure float64 algebra: analyticT() is a quadratic intercept solve, wrapAwareT() is a min over the 9 nearest torus images. NO `t<=TMAX` loop exists in the feature or pick path (grep: TMAX only used for clipping at lines 58,60,61). The exact O(N·TMAX) scan eg_scan.scanT (loop `for t=0..TMAX`) lives ONLY in repro/eg_scan.js (training labels) and is required in candidates/egnn.js solely at line 105 to compute prodIdx for measuring disagreement — it never enters the NN snapshot (egnn.js:108-110 builds {px,py,pvx,pvy,psize,bx,by,bvx,bvy} = raw kinematics only). endgame/eg_features.js, egboidPick.js, eg_scan.js are byte-identical (diff) to the student source.

findings.issue: Graceful degradation independently re-run on a 4.6x larger held-out sample — rules out both exact-answer relay and passthrough/noise
findings.evidence: I re-ran verifier/run_egnn_ablation.sh at 60 seeds x 6 cells (1141 commits, vs verdict's 246) on the held-out [270000,280000) calibration range (non-sealed). Results: oracle=100.000% (egDis=0), full NN=99.387% (egDis=7), -wa0=96.144% (egDis=44), -analytic[10,12,13,14]=80.368% (egDis=224), -reach[10..15] pure raw pos/vel=81.858% (egDis=207), raw_geom argmin(wa0) NO-NN=99.211% (egDis=9). Monotone decline; never stuck at ~100% (not an exact relay), never collapses to noise (~82% on pure kinematics, well above random). Verified the ablation monkeypatch targets the SAME eg_features.js module instance egboidPick consumes (shared require cache, confirmed via require.resolve), and the feature index map (12=wa0 wrap-aware analytic time; 10-15=analytic/reach; 0-9=raw rel/abs pos+vel survive `reach`) is exactly as the harness claims.

findings.issue: Honest caveat: the NN's value-add over the user-authorized analytic prior argmin(wa0) is marginal (~0.18pp) — disclosed, not a hole
findings.evidence: On my 1141-commit sample, full NN=99.387% (7 disagreements) vs no-NN argmin(wa0)=99.211% (9 disagreements): a +0.175pp delta. On easy separable endgame commits the NN is near-equivalent to a single closed-form feature. However: (1) the closed-form analytic wrap-aware reach time is EXPLICITLY user-authorized as a learnable feature; (2) the gate is cheap-geom decisions clearing >=95%, which holds honestly (per-cell all >=98.9% sealed); (3) PHASE2_ENDGAME_VERDICT.md lines 67-71 disclose this exact nuance up-front rather than overstating the NN. This is a transparency-preserving caveat about the easy geometry, not a cheat or an overstated decider.

angles.conclusion: CLAIM SURVIVES. The NN genuinely decides from allowed cheap closed-form geometry, NOT from prod's exact O(N·TMAX) scan-t — proven three independent ways: (1) source audit (no t<=TMAX loop in the feature/pick path; eg_scan is training-labels-only in repro/ and a disagreement-measurement aside in the harness, never an input); (2) oracle control = exactly 100% while the NN is not; (3) my own ablation on a 4.6x larger held-out sample (1141 commits) reproduces graceful degradation (99.39% -> 96.14% -> ~82% as analytic-reach features are progressively zeroed), which rules out both an exact-answer relay (would stay ~100%) and a single-value passthrough / noise collapse. The one honest caveat — the NN's value-add over the user-authorized argmin(wa0) analytic prior is only ~0.18pp on this easy separable geometry — is real but is explicitly disclosed in the verdict (lines 67-71) and does not undermine the genuineness claim, since the analytic reach feature is authorized and the cheap-geom decision clears >=95% honestly. No material hole. Severity: low.

angles.claim: The harness measures endgame S_dec faithfully: oracle=100%, egBoid-identity matches prod, disagreements counted per-decision (resync), per-cell real, merge exact from raw counts, 0 malformed, fresh-salt weights SHA pinned.

findings.issue: verdict_moe.js reads global.__moeStatsLast but candidates/egnn.js writes global.__egnnStatsLast — a stats-global name mismatch. For every egnn run __moeStatsLast is UNDEFINED, so the gateFlips/gateMalformed accumulators (verdict_moe.js:112) never receive egnn's counters. The 'gateMalformed_total:0' and 'gateFlips_total:0' fields in BOTH committed merged JSONs (and all 10 shards) are therefore vacuous constants, NOT measurements of the candidate. The verdict_moe selftest's calibration self-proof (egDisagree==gateFlips) only exercises moe.js (which DOES write __moeStatsLast), giving false assurance that this cross-check covers the egnn candidate.
findings.evidence: grep: verdict_moe.js:108 `const gs=global.__moeStatsLast`; egnn.js:125 `global.__egnnStatsLast=stats`. Direct probe over 16 sealed-overlap games: global.__moeStatsLast=UNDEFINED while __egnnStatsLast={flips:0,malformed:0,...}. In a perturb run with REAL flips the reported gateFlips_total stayed 0 (egDisagree=2) — the 'egDisagree==gateFlips' calibration is structurally unsatisfiable for egnn via this driver.

findings.issue: Undisclosed n==1 sole-boid inflation of the endgame S_dec denominator. ~28-30% of endgame gate commits have a single boid, where the NN's argmin is forced to index 0 == prod (trivial, cannot disagree) yet still counts in the denominator. The PHASE2_ENDGAME_VERDICT.md headline (99.42% scatter) does not disclose this. Excluding n==1, the non-trivial (n>=2) S_dec is 99.08% vs reported 99.34% on my 6-cell/48-seed sealed-overlap sample — real but small (~0.26pp). Non-blocking (clears 95% with large margin even stripped), but the task explicitly required this disclosure and it is absent.
findings.evidence: Per-gate-call stratified probe (6 cells x 48 sealed seeds, 910 gate calls): n=1 calls=255 (28.0%) disagree=0 S_dec=1.000; n>=2 calls=655 disagree=6 S_dec=0.99084 vs all-N S_dec=0.99341. A separate 2-cell run showed soleN1/commits=29.9%. The harness egCommits (910) equals the per-gate-call total (910) exactly, confirming the denominator is real but includes the forced-agree n==1 events.

findings.issue: The local endgame/eg_weights.json (sha 5fb87949…) differs from the weights actually evaluated and SHA-pinned in the merged reports (f84abae2…, the student drop). Not a measurement error — the committed reports correctly pin the evaluated file — but the in-tree default weights are a stale/divergent artifact that would silently change results if someone re-ran without --weights.
findings.evidence: sha256(endgame/eg_weights.json)=5fb87949e848… ; sha256(student-drop eg_weights.json)=f84abae26a9f… == both merged JSONs' weights_sha256. Hand-recompute of S_dec from raw totals matches reported to 9 d.p. (scatter 1-56/9615=0.994175767; natural 1-5/29232=0.999828955).

probes_run: see above findings/probes_run list
angles.conclusion: The core measurement is faithful and the ≥95% gate survives. Oracle=100% (perfect agreement + zero trajectory divergence) and perturb (real flips drop S_dec and diverge trajectories) bracket the instrument's calibration; crucially, the harness egDisagree exactly equals egnn's own flip/disagree counters at the source, so the PRIMARY S_dec metric (derived from the harness decision-level comparison, NOT the candidate stats) is genuine and per-decision. Re-merging the committed shards reproduces the headline numbers exactly and hand arithmetic matches to 9 d.p.; weights SHA is pinned to the evaluated file; sealed salt matches its commitment. Two medium defects qualify the claim rather than break it: (1) a stats-global name mismatch (__moeStatsLast vs __egnnStatsLast) makes the reported gateMalformed_total/gateFlips_total fields VACUOUS for every egnn run — they are 0 by accident of an UNDEFINED read, not by measurement; the '0 malformed' assertion is nonetheless TRUE (independently confirmed = 0 at the source), and the verdict_moe selftest's egDisagree==gateFlips calibration only ever validated the MoE candidate, not egnn; (2) the n==1 sole-boid fraction (~28-30%) inflates the endgame denominator with forced agreements and is undisclosed — the non-trivial n>=2 S_dec is ~99.08% vs the reported ~99.34%, a small but real gap. Both are honesty/transparency gaps, not gate-threatening: even excluding all n==1 commits and treating the malformed field as unmeasured, the NN clears 95% with very large margin and oracle/perturb prove the instrument is sound. PASS-with-caveats.

angles.claim: The Phase-2 sealed result is a clean one-shot: FRESH salt bac52d51 (distinct from the Phase-1-REVEALED 2f2ee894), pre-registered in seal_commitment_p2.json before side-a's model, sealed seeds >=290000, held-out approx equal to sealed (generalizes), training excludes sealed seeds.

findings.issue: Salt matches its commitment and is genuinely fresh. seal_seeds.js --verify (EXACTNN_SALT_PATH=~/.exactnn_seal_salt_p2, commit=seal_commitment_p2.json) returns MATCH: sha256(salt)=bac52d51...=committed. p2 salt sha bac52d51... != Phase-1 public salt sha 2f2ee894... (the Phase-1 salt_hex+seeds ARE exposed in evidence/seal_reveal_audit_trail.json, so re-sealing was necessary and was done correctly).

findings.issue: Anti-circularity holds at the DATA level: sealed seeds are provably absent from all training and calibration data. Computed all 4096 sealed seeds from the p2 salt and intersected with every training/calibration seed; intersection = 0. Train+calib seed range is [100000,272249], entirely below the sealed pool FLOOR=290000. This is the substantive guarantee that the model could not be tuned to the verification set.
findings.evidence: Script over packed_eg.json.gz / packed_eg_mix.json.gz / packed_eg_big.json.gz (seeds 100000-120179) + data_eg + calib_eg_nat (seed 272000): 'SEALED INTERSECT (train+calib) = 0; train+calib seed range: [100000,272249] (FLOOR for sealed pool=290000)'. eg_pack.js itself applies NO seed filter, so the separation rests entirely on which seeds were generated into the shards -- verified empirically here.

findings.issue: Commitment pre-dates the verdict NUMBERS, but the verdict's literal wording 'side-a model frozen before [the commitment]' is wall-clock inaccurate. The commitment was committed 2026-06-14 20:00:08; the PIVOT to the pure endgame NN (20:43-20:47) and the model weights (21:24:46, sha f84abae2) were created AFTER the commitment. This does NOT break anti-circularity (sealed seeds were secret, HMAC-underivable without the chmod-600 salt, and absent from training -- all verified), but the specific freeze-ordering claim as phrased is false and should be corrected to 'sealed seeds were secret and disjoint from training' rather than 'model frozen before commitment'.
findings.evidence: git timeline: 'FRESH sealed salt ... pre-register commitment' 20:00:08; 'PURE cheap-geom endgame NN, no fallback' 21:24:46; sealed scatter verdict 22:22:39; reveal 23:07:59. Model mtime 21:23 > commitment 20:00. The protection is data-separation (verified intersection=0), not the stated chronology.

findings.issue: Held-out generalizes to sealed (no collapse). Held-out NAT-VAL agree (calibration-band natural seeds, never trained) = 98.95%; sealed endgame S_dec scatter = 99.42% (9615 commits, 56 disagree); sealed endgame S_dec natural = 97.52% (202 commits, 5 disagree). Consistent 97.5-99.4% across held-out and sealed -- the model does not degrade off the calibration distribution.
findings.evidence: eg_weights.json nat_val_agree=0.98953; egnn_sealed_scatter_MERGED.json S_dec_endgame=0.994176; egnn_sealed_natural_MERGED.json S_dec_endgame=0.975248. 0 gateMalformed in both.

findings.issue: No salt leak and seeds derive deterministically from the committed salt at offset 0 (not cherry-picked). p2 salt is chmod 600 in side-b home only; its raw hex appears in NO file under /workspace or /shared except side-b's own verdict-time reveal block (egnn_seal_reveal_p2.json), and is absent from side-a's exact-nn-endgame-student/ entirely. Harness-derived sealed seeds (seal.sealedSeeds slice [offset,offset+n]) match the reveal block's first 20, all >=290000.

angles.conclusion: The sealed-discipline claim SURVIVES. All five core sub-claims check out: (1) the p2 salt cryptographically matches its pre-registered commitment (bac52d51) and is genuinely fresh, distinct from the now-public Phase-1 salt 2f2ee894; (2) the commitment was committed before any verdict numbers; (3) sealed seeds are all >=290000 and are PROVABLY disjoint from training+calibration data (computed intersection = 0; train+calib range [100000,272249] sits entirely below the 290000 floor); (4) held-out 98.95% approx sealed 99.42%/97.52% (generalizes, no collapse); (5) the secret salt is chmod-600, never leaked to any side-a-visible path, and the evaluated seeds derive deterministically from it at offset 0 (not cherry-picked). One LOW-severity correction: the verdict's literal phrasing 'side-a model frozen before the commitment' is wall-clock false -- the pivot and model weights (21:24) post-date the commitment (20:00). But anti-circularity does not depend on that chronology; it depends on the sealed seeds being secret and absent from training, both of which I verified empirically. The wording should be tightened, but the sealed result is a sound one-shot. No material hole.

synthesis:
That clears it. The only `eg_bound` mention in `egnn.js` is in a **comment** (line 3): *"committed by the NN's OWN argmax (no eg_bound cert, no scan fallback...)"* — i.e., it documents the *absence* of the cert. So `eg_bound.js` is genuinely not invoked in the egnn decision path; angle-1's wording ("referenced by NOTHING in deploy/decision/egnn") is slightly imprecise (a comment mentions it) but the substance holds: no cert call. The decision path is `r.egIdx` from the NN argmin; the cheap-geom argmin (line 33) is the `raw_geom` ablation control, not a fallback.

All load-bearing claims are independently verified. I have what I need to write the consolidated verdict.

---

# CONSOLIDATED SIGN-OFF VERDICT — Phase-2 Pure Endgame-NN

## 1. Does the sign-off survive all 4 angles?

**Yes — all four angles return `survives: true`, and I independently re-confirmed each angle's load-bearing claim at the source.** No angle was rubber-stamped:

| Angle | Survives | What I re-verified myself |
|---|---|---|
| no-hidden-fallback | YES | Deploy chain (`eg_features.js`→`egboidPick.js`→`endgamePolicy.js`) has **no** exact-scan loop, no `egPick`, no `eg_bound` cert call. The lone `eg_scan.egPick` is at `egnn.js:22` computing `prodIdx` (the disagreement *label*), never an input or fallback. Malformed→`(prodIdx+1)%n` penalty confirmed at `egnn.js:31`. |
| genuineness-no-exact-scant | YES | Decision is `egIdx = argmin over scanTPred` (the MLP forward), `egboidPick.js:43-63`. `TMAX` in `eg_features.js` is used only for clipping (lines 58,60,61), not a scan loop. Deploy `egboidPick.js`/`eg_features.js` are **byte-identical** (`diff -q` IDENTICAL) to the audited student source. |
| measurement-integrity | YES (with caveats) | Stats-global mismatch confirmed: `verdict_moe.js:108` reads `__moeStatsLast`; `egnn.js:125` writes `__egnnStatsLast`. Weights SHA `f84abae2…` matches the pinned value. |
| sealed-discipline | YES | Fresh salt, sealed-seed/training intersection = 0, floor 290000 > train+calib range [100000,272249]. |

## 2. Material findings (severity ≥ medium)

Two distinct medium findings, both flagged by **multiple independent angles** (a good sign the audit is real, not collusive):

- **F1 — Stats-global wiring bug (`__moeStatsLast` vs `__egnnStatsLast`).** → **DISCLOSED LIMITATION, not a blocker.** The `gateMalformed_total:0` / `gateFlips_total:0` fields in the merged JSONs are *vacuous constants* (an undefined read), not measurements. Critically, this does **not** create a hidden fallback: a malformed pick still surfaces as `egDisagree` and lowers S_dec (proven: malformed student → 27.8% S_dec, 26/36 disagree). The "0 malformed" fact is *independently true* for the real model; only its *provenance in the JSON* is broken. The PRIMARY S_dec metric is derived from the harness decision-level comparison, **not** these stats fields, so the headline number is unaffected.

- **F2 — Undisclosed n==1 sole-boid denominator inflation (~28–30%).** → **DISCLOSED LIMITATION, not a blocker.** Single-boid commits force `argmin = index 0 = prod` (cannot disagree) yet count in the denominator. Stripping them: non-trivial n≥2 S_dec ≈ 99.08% vs reported ≈ 99.34% — a real but small ~0.26pp gap. Surfaced only in prose, not in the sealed numbers. The gate clears 95% with enormous margin even fully stripped.

**Blockers: none.** Neither finding touches the architectural claim (no cert, no scan fallback, planner verbatim) or the anti-circularity guarantee.

One wording nit (severity low, worth a correction): angle-1 says `eg_bound` is "referenced by NOTHING in egnn" — in fact `egnn.js:3` mentions it in a **comment** asserting its absence. Substance holds (no cert *call*), phrasing is imprecise. Also (sealed angle): the verdict's "model frozen before commitment" is wall-clock false (model 21:24 > commitment 20:00) — anti-circularity rests on sealed-seed *secrecy + data disjointness* (intersection=0, verified), not chronology. Wording should be tightened.

## 3. The single most important honest caveat

**The NN's measured value-add over the user-authorized closed-form analytic prior `argmin(wa0)` is marginal — roughly +0.18pp (7 vs 9 disagreements over 1141 held-out commits).** The endgame geometry on these scatter/natural distributions is easy and largely separable by a single authorized feature (wrap-aware analytic reach time), so a no-NN analytic argmin already reaches ~99.2%. The NN is genuine and decides from allowed cheap-geom — it is *not* an exact-answer relay (ablation degrades monotonically 99.4%→96.1%→~82%, never stuck at 100%, never collapsing to noise) — but on this data it is *barely better than the closed-form floor it's allowed to use*. This is honestly disclosed in `PHASE2_ENDGAME_VERDICT.md` (lines 67-71), so it is a transparency-preserving caveat, not a cheat.

## 4. Bottom line

**PASS-with-caveats.**

---

### KEY question — Is this endgame NN GENUINE? Did the pivot away from the unified-MoE cheat produce an honest deciding NN?

**Yes — genuinely, on the architecture; with one honesty asterisk on its *significance*.**

The pivot is real and verifiable. The deployed decision is purely the NN's argmin over `scanTPred` (the MLP forward) computed from 18-dim **cheap closed-form geometry** in `eg_features.js` — confirmed three independent ways: (a) source audit shows no `t<=TMAX` scan loop, no `egPick`, no `eg_bound` cert anywhere in the `eg_features→egboidPick→endgamePolicy` path; (b) the oracle control hits exactly 100% while the NN does not, proving the NN is a *separate* predictor from prod's exact scan; (c) ablation degrades gracefully (99.4%→~82% as analytic features are zeroed), ruling out both an exact-answer relay and a passthrough/noise stub. Prod's exact O(N·TMAX) scan (`eg_scan.egPick`) lives *only* in training-label generation and as a disagreement-*measurement* aside in the harness — it never enters the net's input and never serves as a decision fallback. A malformed pick is genuinely penalized as a disagreement, not silently rerouted to prod. The planner (N>5) is byte-verbatim prod (planDisagree 0/2297, force mismatches 0). Anti-circularity is sound: sealed seeds are secret, HMAC-underivable, and provably disjoint from all training/calibration data (intersection = 0).

So: the unified-MoE cheat (where the "NN" was effectively relaying the exact answer) is genuinely gone. **The honest asterisk** is not about whether it cheats — it doesn't — but about *how much it earns its keep*: on this easy, separable endgame geometry the NN beats a single authorized closed-form feature by only ~0.18pp. It is an honest, allowed-input deciding NN that clears the ≥95% gate cleanly; it is just not a *dramatically* better decider than the closed-form prior it is permitted to use. That nuance is disclosed up-front in the verdict, which is exactly what a clean sign-off should do.

**Verdict: PASS-with-caveats. The endgame NN is genuine; sign off, with the two medium measurement-integrity defects (vacuous gate-stats fields, undisclosed n==1 denominator inflation) and the ~0.18pp value-add caveat recorded as disclosed limitations — none are blockers.**
